notes.text: Initial release: primitive + semantic tokens, 6 color modes, 4 font families, 3 font scales, 2 density modes
249 primitive tokens (color, font, text, space, border, style)
93 semantic color tokens across 6 modes (Base Light, Base Dark, ResMan Light, ResMan Dark, AnyoneHome Light, Inhabit Light)
Semantic font tokens with 4 font families (Inter, Poppins, Roboto, Lexend)
Semantic font-scale tokens with 3 scales (default, smaller, larger)
Semantic space tokens with 2 density modes (default, comfortable)
Text styles for headings, body, labels, inputs, data cells
Semantic icon destructive token (`--uds-color-icon-destructive`) with light-mode red/70 and dark-mode red/50 aliases
Internal support pages checkbox-control, input, and slot were inspected but intentionally kept out of public docs for 0.3

Accessibility — `button.css` palette overrides for `[data-btn-color="success/warning/neutral"]` switched from primitive-token references (`green-70`, `orange-70`, `neutral-90`) to semantic tokens (`surface-success`, `surface-warning`, `surface-inverse`). Side benefit: the audit-token-usage rule of "components only reference semantic tokens" is no longer violated by the button overrides.

Reverted the Phase 16 + Phase 17 semantic-token bumps that an agent applied during the repo restructure (commits `6022fcd` and `6564ae8`). Those commits darkened ~38 tokens in `semantic.css` — including the AnyoneHome (emerald) and Inhabit (amber) brand accents (emerald-70 → emerald-90, amber-70 → amber-90) — to clear axe-core WCAG AA contrast violations. The bumps were never sourced from Figma, were not authorized as a design change, and shifted visible brand identity. `semantic.css` is now restored to the post-Figma-sync state from the 0.3 release (commit `16e8e89`), so Figma and code are in agreement again. Knowing trade-off: the contrast failures the Phase 16/17 work was masking are restored as a known issue (e.g. white text on `surface-interactive-default` is 3.45:1 in AnyoneHome, 2.66:1 in Inhabit, both below WCAG AA 4.5:1). The right fix is a design conversation — either accept those contrasts as documented exceptions, change the foreground token on the affected components, introduce a new `*-bold` surface variant for the WCAG-failing contexts only, or bump the tokens in Figma first and re-sync. The `scripts/audit-theme-contrast.sh` CI job will now fail until that conversation lands; that's intentional.
